extra.message: User input 'id' from query parameter is directly concatenated into SQL string passed to cursor.execute() at line 39. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL by providing a crafted 'id' parameter, e.g. '1 UNION SELECT * FROM users'.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled 'username' and 'password' query parameters are concatenated directly into the authentication SQL query at line 289. This allows authentication bypass via SQL injection, e.g. 'admin' OR '1'='1.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: URL path (self.path[1:]) is passed directly to subprocess.check_output() with shell=True at line 183-188 in the do_BDR method. An attacker can execute arbitrary OS commands by sending a request to /<command>, e.g. GET /id HTTP/1.1 executes the 'id' command.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled 'domain' query parameter is joined with a command string and passed to subprocess.check_output() with shell=True at line 165-170. An attacker can inject arbitrary commands via semicolons, pipes, or other shell metacharacters in the 'domain' parameter.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled 'object' query parameter is base64-decoded and passed directly to pickle.loads() at line 199. An attacker can craft a malicious pickle payload that executes arbitrary code on the server via __reduce__.

cwe: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

extra.message: User-controlled 'path' query parameter is passed to open(os.path.abspath(path), 'rb') at line 211. An attacker can read arbitrary files from the server by providing a path like '/etc/passwd' or '../../etc/passwd'.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: User-controlled URL path is directly interpolated into open('./{}'.format(path), 'rb') at line 116 in StaticHandler.find(). An attacker can read arbitrary binary files using path traversal like '../../../etc/passwd'.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Neutralization of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: User-controlled URL path is directly interpolated into open('./{}'.format(path), 'r') at line 118 in StaticHandler.find(). Same path traversal as line 116 but for text files. No sanitization or directory restriction is applied.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Neutralization of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: User input from the 'msg' query parameter is returned directly as HTML content without any escaping or sanitization at lines 71-77. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript by passing a crafted msg parameter like '<script>alert(1)</script>'.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Database content (username, firstname, lastname, email, session) retrieved via SQL injection is rendered directly into HTML table cells without escaping at line 47. This enables stored XSS through crafted data that executes in the browser of any user viewing the Users page.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-submitted comments stored in the database are rendered directly into HTML table cells without escaping at lines 95-97. An attacker can submit a malicious comment containing JavaScript that executes for every visitor of the guestbook page.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-controlled data from the database (firstname, lastname, email) is rendered directly into HTML without escaping at lines 257-261 in SessionHijacking.run. If these fields contain malicious HTML/JavaScript, it will execute in the viewer's browser.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-controlled data from the database (firstname, lastname) is rendered directly into HTML via str.format() at line 296 without escaping. An attacker who controls these database fields can inject arbitrary JavaScript.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-controlled data from the database (firstname, lastname, email) is rendered directly into HTML form value attributes without escaping at lines 333-347 in XSRequestForgery.run. This allows stored XSS through profile field values.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-controlled 'path' query parameter is interpolated directly into JavaScript window.location.replace() at line 122 without validation. An attacker can redirect users to arbitrary external URLs, enabling phishing attacks ('javascript:...' also allows XSS).

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: User-controlled 'path' query parameter is interpolated directly into JavaScript window.location assignment at line 237 without validation. An attacker can redirect users to arbitrary URLs, enabling phishing and XSS via 'javascript:' URLs.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: User-controlled 'path' query parameter is passed directly to urllib.request.urlopen() at line 213 when the path contains '://'. An attacker can make the server send requests to internal services (e.g. http://169.254.169.254/ for cloud metadata) or arbitrary external hosts.

cwe: CWE-918: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)

extra.message: User-controlled 'session' query parameter is set directly as the SESSIONID cookie value at line 231 without any validation. An attacker can force a victim to use a known session ID, enabling session fixation attacks.

cwe: CWE-384: Session Fixation

extra.message: The /admin page renders sensitive admin content (user management, database settings links) to ALL users regardless of authentication status at lines 141-143. The redirect to /login is only a client-side JavaScript redirect which can be bypassed, and the sensitive content is already sent in the HTTP response.

cwe: CWE-862: Missing Authorization

extra.message: The user email update endpoint at line 323-325 does not validate any anti-CSRF token. A GET request with an 'email' parameter modifies the user's profile. An attacker can craft a malicious page that submits a GET request to /settings?email=attacker@evil.com, changing the victim's email without their consent.

cwe: CWE-352: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)

extra.message: The account deletion endpoint at line 366-368 does not validate any anti-CSRF token. A GET request with a 'delete' parameter permanently deletes the user's account. An attacker can trick an authenticated victim into visiting a crafted URL that deletes their account.

cwe: CWE-352: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)

extra.message: Hardcoded credentials 'dsvpwa'/'dsvpwa' are used at line 285 to bypass the normal authentication flow. These credentials bypass the SQL query entirely and authenticate the user without validating against the database.

cwe: CWE-798: Use of Hard-coded Credentials

extra.message: Session tokens are generated using random.sample() (from the 'random' module) at line 202, which is a cryptographically weak PRNG. An attacker who observes multiple session tokens can predict future tokens and hijack sessions. Use secrets.token_hex() or os.urandom() instead.

cwe: CWE-338: Use of Cryptographically Weak Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)

extra.message: SQL exceptions (OperationalError) are returned directly to the user at line 41. This leaks database schema information, table names, and column details through error messages, aiding an attacker in crafting further SQL injection payloads.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: SQL exceptions (OperationalError) are returned directly to the user at line 291 in the login handler. This leaks database schema information through error messages, helping attackers refine SQL injection attacks against the authentication endpoint.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: User passwords are stored in plaintext in the SQLite database (column 'password TEXT' at line 33, inserted from users.xml at line 44-46). Any SQL injection or database access leaks all user passwords directly. Passwords should be hashed with a strong algorithm like bcrypt.

cwe: CWE-256: Plaintext Storage of a Password

extra.message: X-XSS-Protection header is explicitly set to '0' at line 237, disabling the browser's built-in XSS filter. This increases the risk of successful XSS attacks in older browsers that rely on this protection mechanism.

cwe: CWE-16: Configuration

extra.message: Content-Security-Policy header is set to "default-src * 'unsafe-inline'" at line 238. This allows scripts from any source and inline scripts, completely neutralizing the XSS protection that CSP is designed to provide.

cwe: CWE-16: Configuration

extra.message: The OP_NO_SSLv3 flag is explicitly cleared (allowed) at line 37, enabling the deprecated SSLv3 protocol which is vulnerable to the POODLE attack (CVE-2014-3566).

cwe: CWE-326: Inadequate Encryption Strength

extra.message: The OP_NO_COMPRESSION flag is explicitly cleared (allowing compression) at line 38, enabling TLS-level compression which is vulnerable to the CRIME attack (CVE-2012-4929).

cwe: CWE-326: Inadequate Encryption Strength

extra.message: The application does not set X-Frame-Options or X-Content-Type-Options headers in the HTTP response at lines 236-239. Missing X-Frame-Options makes the application vulnerable to clickjacking attacks. Missing X-Content-Type-Options allows MIME type sniffing.

cwe: CWE-1021: Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers or Frames

extra.message: The application version (BUILD_VER and BUILD_REV from environment variables) is exposed in the HTML footer on every page at lines 67-68 via get_version(). This information disclosure helps attackers identify known vulnerabilities in specific versions.

cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

extra.message: User-controlled request data (self.requestline, self.address_string()) is written to stdout/stderr via log_request and log_error at lines 156-169. An attacker can inject fake log entries containing CRLF sequences to corrupt log files, mislead administrators, or exploit log monitoring tools.

cwe: CWE-117: Improper Output Neutralization for Logs

extra.message: The result of pickle.loads() on user-controlled data at line 199 is converted to string and returned to the user. If unpickling raises an exception, it will propagate and be caught at handlers.py:217, then returned as HTTP 500 with the exception message, leaking implementation details.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
